meta.note: Live, auto-deployed via GitHub. Update via Avery.

committees.name: Conference Committee
committees.notes: Registration at 160 (35 exhibitors/sponsors). Keynote Rustin Moore signed. PetDesk secured as new sponsor. Conference & pre-conference schedule largely finalized.
committees.nextSteps: Finalize Thursday night community event, book signing, moderator assignments, and Recover session.

committees.name: JAVTE
committees.currentFocus: 2x/year product and growth as a resource
committees.notes: Summer 2026 issue on track. Author cheat sheet created and published with Summer call. Max print length: 20 pages.
committees.nextSteps: Submissions due June 1, review June 1 to 15, print July 20, ship July 24 for conference.

committees.name: Resource Exchange
committees.notes: Platform launched and active for all members. First fireside chat held May 20. Legacy conference materials actively being uploaded. Facebook engagement underway.
committees.nextSteps: Community engagement calendar for rest of 2026. Exploring post-conference collaboration/income opportunity.

committees.name: Veterinary Technician Education Specialist (VTES)
committees.notes: Application now fully online and automated. Points system updated. 2 qualified applicants in process. Committee booth planned for conference.
committees.nextSteps: Finalize updated exam. Execute conference booth. Board encouraged to review updated VTES info on the website.

committees.name: Awards Committee
committees.notes: Full selection cycle complete. All nominees and nominators notified. Cycle closed for the year.
committees.nextSteps: Review award announcement process to improve turnout and engagement in future cycles.

committees.name: Advocacy Committee
committees.currentFocus: Ad hoc response to state-level legislative needs
committees.notes: Proactive outreach to MN programs re: nurse initiative rule-making. One program responded, no support needed. Monitoring ongoing.
committees.nextSteps: Continue monitoring legislative initiatives and support programs that need legislative assistance.

committees.name: Leadership Development Committee
committees.currentFocus: Developing charge and direction; broader vet tech leadership pipeline
committees.notes: Second meeting held. Nominations call is live. Developing a leadership program concept for the broader vet tech organization.
committees.nextSteps: Build out concept document for board feedback.

committees.name: Sponsorship Committee
committees.notes: First meeting held. Charge document created. Sponsor list started from conference roster. Menu-style benefits options under development.
committees.nextSteps: Refine benefits package. Identify and assign sponsor outreach leads ahead of July conference.

committees.name: Fellows in Veterinary Technician Education (FVTE)
committees.notes: Sr. FVTE pilot application launched for beta testing. Virtual Q3 program in development. Seeking board feedback on Sr. FVTE requirements.
committees.nextSteps: Collect beta feedback on Sr. FVTE application and finalize Q3 virtual program plan.

committees.name: Membership Committee
committees.notes: Identified as a need, not yet launched. Recruitment and retention focus. Chair needed.

committees.name: Vet Tech Fall Faculty Academy
committees.currentFocus: Finalizing plan to announce in Portland
committees.notes: Two-day virtual intensive. Launch: week after Labor Day 2026. Cohort: 25 to 30 new vet tech faculty. Fee: $50 to $75. Mentorship through December. Co-Directors confirmed: Rebecca Newman (Appalachian State) and Laura Boarman (Owensboro CC). Third co-director role still needed. Group continues to meet. Identified questions for the program directors survey to aid program development, the questions were included and the results shared back with the volunteer leads.
committees.nextSteps: Use the survey results to shape the program. Board approval of concept. Confirm third Co-Director. Finalize registration fee and marketing plan. Announce in Portland.

committees.name: AVTE Standards Series (CVTEA Webinar Program)
committees.currentFocus: Finalizing program concept to announce in Portland
committees.notes: 11-month virtual webinar series, September to July, one session per CVTEA Accreditation Standard. Karla Lombana has agreed to co-chair. Recruiting a co-chair partner for the effort. Feeds into Resource Exchange discussion threads.
committees.nextSteps: Recruit a co-chair partner to join Karla Lombana. Board discussion and approval. Recruit presenters for Standards 1 to 4. Set up Resource Exchange threads.

committees.name: Career Center
committees.notes: Exit from the existing career center contract secured. Signing the paperwork now, which clears the way to resume production of the new site as part of the AVMA Career Center. W-9 complete, logo submitted, launch call done.
committees.nextSteps: Sign the exit paperwork. Resume production of the new AVMA Career Center site. Complete remaining migration tasks and launch.

committees.name: Merchandise
committees.notes: Transitioning AVTE branded merchandise to a new online store platform.
committees.nextSteps: Complete platform transition and relaunch store.

committees.name: VT Inclusion, Diversity, Equity & Success (VTIDES)
committees.notes: Active grant management. Ongoing initiative, not a formal committee.

committees.name: VTNE Advocacy / External Relations
committees.currentFocus: Multi-org coalition: VTNE/CVTEA crosswalk and AAVSB bylaw changes
committees.notes: Active multi-party partnership with AAVSB, AVMA, NAVTA, CVTEA. VTNE/CVTEA crosswalk completed. Preparing for the July meeting at the AVMA Convention in Anaheim. AVMA has circulated draft materials and a proposed agenda. Canadian VMA joining in July. AAVSB bylaw change proposal went public mid-May 2026. Comms plan active.
committees.nextSteps: Review AVMA's draft materials and proposed agenda ahead of the July meeting. Execute Phase 1 comms plan. Coordinate Portland conference fireside chat.
